title: Fix attention-inbox writer schema in two prompts: emit status:'pending' + recommended_lane:'<lane>' from decider-stale-skip (decider-intake.md Step 1h2 Step 1a) and PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED (analyst-mode1-expansions.md). Prevents the silent-skip / inbox-orphan failure mode observed 2026-05-17 with 6 STALE-ASSESS items.
related_directives: DIRECTIVE-20260517-003 (this PROP's authoring directive — explicitly asks for the 2-line schema patch and bundled-scan of other writers)
DIRECTIVE-20260517-001 (decider Step 1m hotfix — sibling small-fix style)
DIRECTIVE-20260517-002 (analyst claim discipline — different pipeline, similar small-fix style)
related_props: PROP-022 phase 4 (attention-inbox archive convention — same file, sibling cleanup target; established the canonical `status: pending | resolved` field shape that this PROP enforces at write-time)
PROP-028 Mech A (stale-assessment check — the originating mechanism whose writer this PROP fixes; do NOT change the mechanism, only the inbox-item shape)
PROP-029 (orphan-issue safety net in analyst-mode1-expansions.md — the second writer this PROP fixes)
PROP-044 (analyst claim discipline — orthogonal: prevents cross-run duplicate work; does NOT address writer-schema bugs)

headline_recommendation.selected_option: Option A — minimal two-field addition at the two known writer sites. Add `status: 'pending'` and `recommended_lane: '<lane>'` to each writer's object literal. STALE-ASSESS items get `recommended_lane: 'analyst-mode-1b'` (prediction re-assessment work). PROP029-ORPHAN items get `recommended_lane: 'tinker'` (pipeline-decoupling investigation, no specific mode). No dispatcher edits, no consumer-side changes, no schema-version bump on attention-inbox itself.
headline_recommendation.rationale_one_paragraph: The canonical attention-inbox schema (DATA-SCHEMAS.md §analyst/attention-inbox.json) defines `status: 'pending' | 'resolved'` as a required field. The Mode 2b dispatcher in analyst.md filters by `x.status === 'pending'`. Both of the writers patched here emit `resolved: false` with NO `status` field, so their items are silently invisible to the dispatcher filter — they sit in the inbox indefinitely with no consumer (observed 2026-05-17: 6 STALE-ASSESS items lingered until operator-direct commit 3688bbe). Adding `status: 'pending'` restores dispatcher visibility. Adding `recommended_lane` lets Mode 2b (and any future Mode-N dispatcher) classify the item by lane rather than guess from `id` prefix or `created_by` — STALE-ASSESS is Mode 1b semantics (prediction verdict work), PROP029-ORPHAN is tinker-investigation semantics (pipeline diagnostic). The `recommended_lane` value is advisory only; dispatcher semantics are not changed by this PROP (per directive scope guardrail).
headline_recommendation.scoped_to: Two markdown files only: `monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md` (Step 1h2 Step 1a writer, ~lines 240-253) and `monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode1-expansions.md` (Check for Orphaned Issues block, ~lines 68-79). No edits to dispatcher logic in `analyst.md`, no edits to `DATA-SCHEMAS.md`, no edits to existing inbox items (operator already applied the in-session fix to the 6 STALE-ASSESS items in commit 3688bbe).

executive_summary.problem_one_line: Two writers in the agent-prompt set emit attention-inbox items with `resolved: false` and NO `status` field, causing Mode 2b's `status === 'pending'` filter to silently skip them; the items accumulate in the inbox with no consumer until operator-direct cleanup.
executive_summary.evidence_one_line: Observed 2026-05-17: analyst run ~17:30Z surfaced 6 STALE-ASSESS-* items lingering in attention-inbox.json with `resolved: false` and no status; operator-direct commit 3688bbe (2026-05-17) retrofit the missing fields but did NOT fix the writer in decider-intake.md, so the next decider-stale-skip fire will reproduce the bug. A second writer with the same class of bug exists in analyst-mode1-expansions.md ('Check for Orphaned Issues' block) for PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED items.
executive_summary.fix_one_line: At each writer site, replace the single line `resolved: false` with three lines: `status: 'pending'`, `recommended_lane: '<lane>'`, `resolved: false` (kept for backward-compat — historic items use it; future cleanup can drop it once analyst.md and dispatchers no longer reference it). STALE-ASSESS lane = 'analyst-mode-1b'; PROP029-ORPHAN lane = 'tinker'.
executive_summary.shape_one_line: ~6 line-additions total across 2 files. No code logic changes, no dispatcher changes, no test changes (verification is by inspection of next decider-stale-skip / orphan-detection emission, both rare-fire events).

task_1_inspect_current_writer.decider_intake_md_lines_240_253: Confirmed. Step 1h2 Step 1a contains the stale-skip writer inside a fenced ```javascript block. The object literal passed to `appendAttentionInbox({...})` has fields: id, prediction_id, skipped_assessment_file, skipped_assessment_assessed_at, current_verdict_history_last_at, current_verdict, assessment_proposed_verdict, stale_delta_days, action_recommended, created_by:'decider-stale-skip', created_at:new Date().toISOString(), resolved:false. NO `status` field, NO `recommended_lane` field. Confirmed by Grep of `appendAttentionInbox|attention-inbox` across monitor/prompts/ (returns 6 files; this is the only stale-skip writer site).
task_1_inspect_current_writer.second_writer_analyst_mode1_expansions_m: Confirmed. The 'Check for Orphaned Issues' section's example JSON object has fields: id ('PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED-<YYYY-MM-DD>'), detected_at, orphan_count, orphan_iss_ids, orphan_routed_by_run, action_recommended, action_taken_this_run, resolved:false. Same bug class: NO `status` field. Per directive task 3, this is bundled in scope.

decider_intake_md_patch.line_range: ~lines 250-253 (inside the ```javascript fenced block in Step 1h2 Step 1a; the exact line numbers depend on whether prior edits have shifted the writer)
decider_intake_md_patch.after_object_literal_excerpt:
action_recommended: 'Re-run analyst if fresh assessment needed; otherwise leave as-is.',
         created_by: 'decider-stale-skip',
         created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
         status: 'pending',
         recommended_lane: 'analyst-mode-1b',
         resolved: false
       });
decider_intake_md_patch.rationale: STALE-ASSESS items track prediction re-assessment work that was correctly deferred (predictions.json verdict_history newer than the analyst assessment file). The next consumer is the analyst writing a fresh PRED-assessment file via Mode 1b (prediction writeups). The lane label `analyst-mode-1b` directly names that consumer for any future dispatcher that wants to route inbox items by lane. The Mode 2b dispatcher is the wrong consumer — these are not patch-affected-prior-work items; they are stale-source-data items that need fresh analyst attention via the prediction path. `resolved: false` is kept to match historic items and avoid breaking any consumer that reads it (none currently rely on it, but it costs nothing to keep).

analyst_mode1_expansions_md_patch.line_range: ~lines 68-79 (inside the ```json fenced block illustrating the PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED item shape)
analyst_mode1_expansions_md_patch.before_json_excerpt:
{
  "id": "PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED-<YYYY-MM-DD>",
  "detected_at": "<ISO now>",
  "orphan_count": <N>,
  "orphan_iss_ids": [<list>],
  "orphan_routed_by_run": [<unique routed_by_run values from the orphan set>],
  "action_recommended": "tinker investigates: did decider tracker-write fail, or was this a manual operator reroute? Either way, root-cause and patch the decoupling.",
  "action_taken_this_run": "<N> orphans grouped into <M> EXP entries and worked normally; tracker decoupling root-cause TBD.",
  "resolved": false
}
analyst_mode1_expansions_md_patch.after_json_excerpt:
{
  "id": "PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED-<YYYY-MM-DD>",
  "detected_at": "<ISO now>",
  "orphan_count": <N>,
  "orphan_iss_ids": [<list>],
  "orphan_routed_by_run": [<unique routed_by_run values from the orphan set>],
  "action_recommended": "tinker investigates: did decider tracker-write fail, or was this a manual operator reroute? Either way, root-cause and patch the decoupling.",
  "action_taken_this_run": "<N> orphans grouped into <M> EXP entries and worked normally; tracker decoupling root-cause TBD.",
  "status": "pending",
  "recommended_lane": "tinker",
  "resolved": false
}
analyst_mode1_expansions_md_patch.rationale: PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED items are pipeline-handoff diagnostics — analyst observed an ISS in status='assigned-analyst' with no expansion-tracker entry, indicating either a decider tracker-write failure or a manual operator reroute. The action_recommended explicitly names tinker as the investigator. Lane label `tinker` (no mode suffix) matches the existing precedent in attention-inbox-archive.jsonl where two items (ATT-2026-05-17T02:16:31-EXP-407-collision and ATT-2026-05-17T03:13-DUPLICATE-MODE-1-CONCURRENCY) used `recommended_recipient: 'tinker'`. This PROP standardizes on `recommended_lane` as the canonical field name going forward and treats `recommended_recipient` as a historical sibling that can be migrated in a future cleanup PROP if desired.

field_name_opinion.justification: `target_mode` fits operator-directive where the recipient agent is always tinker and only the mode varies (e.g., target_mode: '4'). attention-inbox items have a variable recipient (analyst / tinker / decider) AND sometimes a mode (analyst-mode-1b vs analyst-mode-2b). `recommended_lane` is a single string that encodes both ('analyst-mode-1b', 'tinker', 'decider-intake-step-1h2'), avoiding a two-field combo. `recommended_recipient` already exists in 2 historic archive items but is recipient-only (lacks mode granularity); items routed to a specific analyst mode need the mode suffix to disambiguate. Backward compatibility: no existing consumer reads either field, so introducing `recommended_lane` is non-breaking.

files_inspected.shape: Step A — End-of-Run Step A: Analyst Attention Inbox. Object literal includes `status: 'pending'` already. NO BUG. SKIP.
Step 1h2 Step 1a — stale-assessment writer. `resolved: false`, no `status`. BUG.
Check for Orphaned Issues — PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED writer. `resolved: false`, no `status`. BUG.
Schema documentation. Declares `status: 'pending' | 'resolved'` as canonical. NOT a writer site.
files_inspected.verdict: documentation-only, no action needed (future PROP could add `recommended_lane` to the canonical schema doc — out of scope here)

files_inspected.shape: Archive convention reference table mentions attention-inbox.json but does not contain a writer. NOT a writer site.
Mentions attention-inbox.json as a smart_copy target, no writer logic. NOT a writer site.

task_3_secondary_writers_scan.DUPLICATE_MODE_1_CONCURRENCY_writer_sear: grep -rn 'DUPLICATE-MODE-1-CONCURRENCY|DUPLICATE-MODE' monitor/prompts/ — returns 0 matches. The directive cited 'ATT-*-DUPLICATE-MODE-1-CONCURRENCY' as an example writer to check; the items observed in attention-inbox-archive.jsonl (id ATT-2026-05-17T03:13-DUPLICATE-MODE-1-CONCURRENCY) were created by analyst Mode 1 self-emission per its own summary, NOT by a templated writer in the prompts. The shape used `recommended_recipient: 'tinker'` (no `recommended_lane`, but with `status: 'pending'`) — already dispatcher-visible, just on a different field convention. No fix needed in the prompts; the items themselves are already terminal.
task_3_secondary_writers_scan.summary: Two writer-site bugs found and patched in this PROP. One historical item-shape divergence (recommended_recipient vs recommended_lane) noted but out of scope per directive guardrail 'do not change Mode 1b vs Mode 2b dispatcher semantics.'

task_4_counter_arguments.counter: Steelman: just delete the writer entirely. STALE-ASSESS items have no current consumer; the action_recommended text is the 'consumer' (operator reads, acts). Removing the inbox emit and instead pushing a one-line log to latest-decider-summary.txt would achieve the same operator-visibility with less file thrash.
task_4_counter_arguments.rebuttal: Two reasons to keep the inbox emit: (1) the operator wanted persistent inbox surfacing (per directive bug_summary: 'remain visible in the inbox' framed as the desired end-state, not a bug to remove); (2) inbox items survive across decider runs and across summary-file overwrites, while a one-line note in latest-decider-summary.txt is overwritten on next decider run (~6x/day per the latest cadence). Three of the six observed STALE-ASSESS items date from 2026-05-17 alone — the inbox accumulates them properly; a summary-line cadence would lose them. Plus: PROP029-ORPHAN items are tinker-investigation work that genuinely needs a queue, not a summary mention. The fix is to make the writer schema-conforming, not to delete the producer.

task_4_counter_arguments.counter: Steelman: why this specific lane label `analyst-mode-1b` instead of just `analyst` or `prediction-reassessment` (semantic-meaningful) or `Mode-1b` (mode-only)? The lane should be agent-agnostic and value-semantic.
task_4_counter_arguments.rebuttal: Two competing pressures: (a) value-semantic labels (e.g., `prediction-reassessment`) are clearer in isolation but require every new consumer to know the full label set; (b) agent-mode labels (e.g., `analyst-mode-1b`) tie directly to the existing dispatcher language in analyst.md ('Mode 1b — Prediction Writeups') so a future dispatcher upgrade can grep for the lane and route mechanically. Agent-mode wins because it preserves the dispatcher invariant — every lane string corresponds to a real consumer at a real path. Pure-agent labels (e.g., `analyst`) lose the mode disambiguation: an `analyst` lane could mean Mode 1 (expansions), Mode 1b (predictions), Mode 2b (attention-inbox-itself), or Mode 3 (defense neutralization). Mode-only labels (e.g., `Mode-1b`) lose the agent disambiguation: which agent runs `Mode-1b`? (Only analyst right now, but if analyst-baby grows a Mode 1b path, the label becomes ambiguous.) `analyst-mode-1b` is the unambiguous canonical pointer. Same logic for `tinker` on PROP029-ORPHAN — tinker has multiple modes (Pipeline Health / Infrastructure / Cost Engineering / Proposals) but the action_recommended text already routes within tinker (investigates), so the bare-agent label is fine for now and can be tightened to `tinker-mode-1` (Pipeline Health) in a future PROP if the operator wants finer routing.

task_4_counter_arguments.counter: Steelman: keep `resolved: false` and DROP `status: 'pending'`. The historical (pre-PROP-022) shape used `resolved: false` and a Mode 2b dispatcher could simply switch its filter from `status === 'pending'` to `!resolved || status === 'pending'` to accept both shapes.
task_4_counter_arguments.rebuttal: Two reasons to add `status` rather than fork the dispatcher: (1) DATA-SCHEMAS.md and PROP-022 phase 4 canonized `status: 'pending' | 'resolved'` as the schema; reverting the dispatcher to dual-accept would re-open the schema and make future writers ambiguous about which field is authoritative; (2) the archive-on-resolve pattern in analyst.md (the canonical pattern documented in state-file-archives.md) writes `item.status = 'resolved'` at archive-time — the live file always carries items with `status` set after one resolution-event cycle. Items born without `status` are out-of-pattern from the start. The minimal-correct fix is to give them the field at write time.

phase_1_design.scope: Two markdown-file edits, one writer-block each. Total ~6 line-additions. No code logic changes.

patch_decider_intake_md.edit_strategy:
Use a targeted Edit tool call. old_string is the trailing 3 lines of the existing object literal:
```
         created_by: 'decider-stale-skip',
         created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
         resolved: false
```
new_string adds two lines between created_at and resolved:
```
         created_by: 'decider-stale-skip',
         created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
         status: 'pending',
         recommended_lane: 'analyst-mode-1b',
         resolved: false
```
patch_decider_intake_md.uniqueness_check: The string `created_by: 'decider-stale-skip'` is unique in decider-intake.md (verified via Grep). The edit is unambiguous.

patch_analyst_mode1_expansions_md.edit_strategy:
Use a targeted Edit tool call. old_string is the trailing 3 lines of the JSON example:
```
  "action_taken_this_run": "<N> orphans grouped into <M> EXP entries and worked normally; tracker decoupling root-cause TBD.",
  "resolved": false
}
```
new_string adds two lines between action_taken_this_run and resolved:
```
  "action_taken_this_run": "<N> orphans grouped into <M> EXP entries and worked normally; tracker decoupling root-cause TBD.",
  "status": "pending",
  "recommended_lane": "tinker",
  "resolved": false
}
```
patch_analyst_mode1_expansions_md.uniqueness_check: The string `"action_taken_this_run": "<N> orphans grouped into <M> EXP entries` is unique in analyst-mode1-expansions.md (verified via Grep). The edit is unambiguous.

phase_1_design.deployment: Operator + cowork-claude apply via tinker's standard PROP-application flow (edits land in the clean clone, `node build.js publish` syncs to FUSE and pushes to git on next workspace-sync cycle). Tinker does NOT self-apply prompt edits per directive scope_authority guardrail.

regression_check.what_will_break_if_we_skip_this_fix: Next decider-stale-skip fire (any analyst PRED-assessment-*.json arriving for a prediction whose verdict_history has been updated since assessed_at): writer emits new STALE-ASSESS-* item with `resolved: false` and no `status`. Mode 2b filter (`x.status === 'pending'`) silently skips it. Item lingers in inbox until operator-direct cleanup. Repeats indefinitely.
regression_check.what_will_break_if_we_apply_this_fix_wro: If `status: 'pending'` is added but `recommended_lane` is set wrong (e.g., 'analyst-mode-2b' instead of 'analyst-mode-1b'), Mode 2b dispatcher would treat the item as its own work and attempt to OBE-triage it. Mode 2b's current OBE criteria reference closed-issues and expansion-tracker — neither would dismiss a STALE-ASSESS item (no ISS-N or EXP-N reference matches), so it would fall through to Phase 3 (singleton substantive). Phase 3 would attempt a re-examination but the item is prediction-not-section work; the analyst would likely write a generic resolved-mark without doing the actual prediction re-assessment. Result: false-resolution + work-loss. Mitigation: PROP explicitly specifies the correct lane labels.
regression_check.what_could_go_wrong_at_apply_time: (a) Edit string-match fails if another agent has touched the writer between PROP authoring and operator application. Mitigation: the chosen old_strings are 3-line blocks unique to each writer (verified via Grep at authoring time). (b) JSON example block in analyst-mode1-expansions.md is illustrative, not executable code — the analyst Mode 0 procedure that emits these items lives elsewhere. Need to verify the analyst-side emit code uses the example shape literally. Mitigation: grep `PROP029-ORPHAN` across the prompts directory to confirm; if a separate emit code block exists, patch it too.

phase_2_optional_followup.scope: OUT OF SCOPE for this PROP. Listed for operator awareness only.
items: Update DATA-SCHEMAS.md §analyst/attention-inbox.json to add `recommended_lane: 'analyst-mode-1b' | 'analyst-mode-2b' | 'tinker' | 'tinker-mode-N' | 'decider-intake' | null` as an optional advisory field. Document the enum and the per-item-writer responsibility to declare it.

Migrate the 2 historical archive items that used `recommended_recipient: 'tinker'` (ATT-2026-05-17T02:16:31-EXP-407-collision, ATT-2026-05-17T03:13-DUPLICATE-MODE-1-CONCURRENCY) by adding `recommended_lane` as a sibling field, then deprecate `recommended_recipient`.

Teach Mode 2b dispatcher to filter by `recommended_lane` so out-of-lane items don't trip the OBE/batch/singleton phases (current Mode 2b would attempt to process all pending items regardless of lane; this PROP's fix makes them visible but does not protect them from wrong-lane processing — currently mitigated by the fact that no analyst-mode-1b item resembles a Mode-2b OBE candidate, so the singleton phase would route them upward to operator review).
phase_2_optional_followup.why_deferred: Directive scope_authority guardrail: 'Do NOT change Mode 1b vs Mode 2b dispatcher semantics. The dispatcher is correct; only the writer schema needs fixing.' Phase 2 is dispatcher work; this PROP is writer work.

verification_pattern.description: How to verify this PROP landed correctly. Tinker should run this on the next post-application run to confirm FIXED.

check_1_writer_decider_intake.expected: Should return a line containing `recommended_lane: 'analyst-mode-1b'` near the stale-skip writer (within ~5 lines of `created_by: 'decider-stale-skip'`).
check_1_writer_decider_intake.failure_mode: Empty grep result → patch not applied to decider-intake.md.

check_2_writer_analyst_mode1.expected: Should return a line containing `"recommended_lane": "tinker"` near the orphan-detected JSON example (within ~5 lines of `PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED`).
check_2_writer_analyst_mode1.failure_mode: Empty grep result → patch not applied to analyst-mode1-expansions.md.

check_3_next_emit_conforms.command: After next decider-stale-skip OR PROP029-orphan fire, inspect the new attention-inbox item: `node -e "const a=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json','utf8'));const newest=a[a.length-1];console.log(newest.id,newest.status,newest.recommended_lane)"`
check_3_next_emit_conforms.failure_mode: Either field missing or empty → the prompt edit was string-matched wrong or there is a SECOND writer path not patched.

open_questions_for_operator: Q1: Confirm field name `recommended_lane` (this PROP's choice) vs `target_mode` (operator-directive convention) vs `recommended_recipient` (historical sibling). I chose `recommended_lane` for the agent+mode unified-string property; willing to flip to either alternative if you prefer consistency with an existing convention.

Q2: Confirm `tinker` (bare agent label) is sufficient for PROP029-ORPHAN, or whether you'd prefer `tinker-mode-1` (Pipeline Health) for finer granularity. Bare label keeps the patch shorter; mode suffix adds dispatcher-routing precision.

Q3: Should we drop the legacy `resolved: false` field from both writers as part of this PROP, since `status: 'pending'` is the canonical replacement? Keeping it costs nothing but adds visual noise; dropping it is a 1-line further-edit per writer. I default to keeping for backward-compat (no consumer currently reads `resolved`, but historical items use it and operator may want symmetric shape between historic and new items).
